expected_evidence.description: Current FSC certificate showing the AGR project remains certified for the crediting period.
expected_evidence.label: FSC Certificate

expected_evidence.description: The FSC certification report referenced by the methodology to evidence certification status.
expected_evidence.label: FSC Certification Report

conditions: The AGR project maintains valid FSC certification throughout the crediting period.
The corresponding FSC certification report is available for certification review.

source_span_text: When applying a dual certification, the Project Developer shall provide the ‘FSC Audit Report’ alongside the Project Design Document (PDD) - the PDD may reference the FSC Audit Report for relevant sections but is not required to duplicate. Project Developers shall also provide the ‘FSC Annual Surveillance Report’ instead of the template for the ‘Annual Report’. For dual certification, FSC certification is required to be valid throughout the crediting period.

expected_evidence.description: Legal instrument showing the project developer holds CO2 user rights or carbon sequestration rights for the project area.
expected_evidence.label: CO2 User Rights Documentation

expected_evidence.description: Land title evidence showing uncontested legal title for the project area during the crediting period.
expected_evidence.label: Uncontested Land Title

expected_evidence.description: Evidence that the project developer owns the timber and non-timber forest product rights for the project area.
expected_evidence.label: Timber and Non-Timber Rights

expected_evidence.description: Required planting, infrastructure, harvesting, or similar permits needed to implement the project.
expected_evidence.label: Implementation Permits

conditions: The project developer holds CO2 user rights or carbon sequestration rights for the project area.
The project developer holds uncontested legal land title for the project area.
The project developer holds timber and non-timber forest product rights for the project area.
All permits needed to implement the project are held and current.

source_span_text: For the duration of the crediting period the Project Developer shall: where a Gold Standard Certified Statement or Product (e.g. GSVERs) is sought, Project Developers must own the CO2 user rights or carbon sequestration rights for the project area, AND hold an uncontested legal land title for the Project Area, AND own the rights for timber and non-timber forest products for the project area, AND hold all necessary permits to implement the project (planting permits, infrastructure permits, harvesting permits, etc.)

expected_evidence.description: Signed agreements between each project participant and the project developer covering transferred CO2 user rights, implementation rights, and uncontested land title or similar entitlement.
expected_evidence.label: Participant Agreements

expected_evidence.description: Contact details plus legal registration and good-standing documentation for each participating organisation, where applicable.
expected_evidence.label: Participant Registration Documentation

expected_evidence.description: Land owner contact details and lease-duration information where those fields apply to the participant agreement package.
expected_evidence.label: Land Owner and Lease Details

conditions: Each project participant signs an agreement with the project developer.
The agreement confirms that the participant holds the CO2 user rights associated with the project activities and has passed them to the project developer.
The agreement confirms that the participant holds the necessary rights to implement the project activities.
The agreement confirms that the legal land title or similar entitlement for the project land is uncontested.
The agreement package includes participant contact details, legal registration and good-standing documentation, and any applicable land-owner or lease details.

source_span_text: The Project Developer acts on behalf of project participants. For such cases, each project participant shall sign an agreement with the Project Developer, which confirms that: (a) The project participant holds the CO2 user rights3 that are associated with the project activities and has passed these on to the Project Developer, AND (b) The project participant holds all necessary rights to implement the project activities (e.g., rights to harvest), AND (c) The legal land title or similar entitlement4 for the land on which the project activities are implemented is uncontested. These agreements shall include the: (a) Contact details of the project participants, AND (b) The legal registration number and documentation by the governing jurisdiction that proves that the entity is in good standing (in case of an organisation), AND (c) Contact details of the land owner (if differing), AND (d) Length of lease contract (if applicable), AND (e) The liabilities and benefits for the person or entity to implement the project activities (e.g., switch to another crop and get access to the seeds).

expected_evidence.description: Evidence that the project developer holds the CO2 user rights and any other certified SDG impact statement or product rights associated with the project activities.
expected_evidence.label: Developer Rights Evidence

expected_evidence.description: Evidence that the project developer holds the rights needed to implement the project activities, such as harvest rights where relevant.
expected_evidence.label: Implementation Rights Evidence

expected_evidence.description: Evidence that the legal land title or similar entitlement for the project land is uncontested.
expected_evidence.label: Uncontested Title Evidence

conditions: The project developer acts on its own rather than on behalf of project participants.
The project developer provides evidence that it holds the CO2 user rights and any associated certified SDG impact statement or product rights.
The project developer provides evidence that it holds the rights needed to implement the project activities.
The project developer provides evidence that the legal land title or similar entitlement for the project land is uncontested.

source_span_text: The Project Developer acts on its own. In such cases, the Project Developer shall provide evidence that: (a) It holds the CO2 user rights5 and the rights for any other Certified SDG Impact Statement or Product that are associated with the project activities, AND (b) It holds all necessary rights to implement the project activities (e.g., rights to harvest), AND (c) The legal land title or similar entitlement for the land on which the project activities are implemented is uncontested.

expected_evidence.description: Assessment of whether the host country accounts for land-based activities under national or subnational GHG accounting.
expected_evidence.label: Host Country Accounting Assessment

expected_evidence.description: Project-specific analysis showing conformance with the GHG Product Requirements Annex A Double Counting Requirements when triggered.
expected_evidence.label: Annex A Double Counting Conformance

conditions: The trigger applies when the host country accounts for its own land-based activities under national or subnational accounting.
When triggered, the project seeking GSVERs conforms to the GHG Emissions Reduction and Sequestration Product Requirements Annex A Double Counting Requirements.

source_span_text: If the host country accounts for greenhouse-gas (GHG) emissions, and hereby accounts for its own land-based activities under its national or subnational accounting, then projects seeking GSVERs shall conform to the GHG Emissions Reduction and Sequestration Product Requirements - Annex A Double Counting Requirements.

expected_evidence.description: Completed Safeguarding Principles assessment prepared for the project area and project region context.
expected_evidence.label: Safeguarding Principles Assessment

expected_evidence.description: Submission record showing the assessment outcome was provided for Preliminary Review.
expected_evidence.label: Preliminary Review Submission

expected_evidence.description: Updated safeguarding assessment package carried forward for Design Certification and Performance Certification.
expected_evidence.label: Certification Update Record

conditions: The safeguarding assessment follows the Safeguarding Principles & Requirements and the Risks & Capacities Guideline.
The assessment considers likely issues in the context of the project region and project area.
The assessment outcome is submitted for Preliminary Review and updated for Design Certification and Performance Certification.

source_span_text: The Project Developer shall conduct the Safeguarding Principles assessment following Safeguarding Principles & Requirements and Risks & Capacities Guideline assessed for the Project Area, taking into account likely issues in the context of the Project Region. The assessment outcome shall be submitted for Preliminary Review and updated as required for Design Certification and Performance Certification.

expected_evidence.description: Map or GIS evidence showing at least 10% of the total project area designated for biodiversity protection or enhancement.
expected_evidence.label: Protected Area Map

expected_evidence.description: Documentation showing the biodiversity designation follows the High Conservation Value approach.
expected_evidence.label: HCV Basis

expected_evidence.description: Evidence that the protected area is within the project area and managed by the project developer.
expected_evidence.label: Protected Area Management Record

conditions: At least 10% of the total project area is identified and used to protect or enhance biological diversity.
The designated area is located within the project area and managed by the project developer.
The designation follows the High Conservation Value approach.
exceptions: Buffer zone areas for water bodies may be included in the designated protected area.

source_span_text: A minimum of 10% of the total Project Area shall be identified and used to protect or enhance the biological diversity following High Conservation Value (HCV) approach. The designated protected area shall be located within the Project Area and managed by the Project Developer. The protected area can include buffer zone areas for water bodies.

expected_evidence.description: Map showing permanent and temporary water bodies with the required 15 metre buffer on both sides.
expected_evidence.label: Water Body Buffer Map

expected_evidence.description: Monitoring or management records showing the prohibited activities are excluded from the buffer zone.
expected_evidence.label: Buffer Zone Controls

conditions: A 15 metre buffer is maintained on both sides of each permanent or temporary water body in scope.
Existing native trees are retained in the buffer zone.
Fertilizer, pesticides, logging, heavy machinery, and cropping are excluded from the buffer zone.
Any trees planted in the buffer zone are native species.
exceptions: Irrigation channels are excluded from the buffer requirement.

source_span_text: The Project Developer shall maintain a buffer zone of 15 meters for water bodies on both sides of any permanent or temporary water bodies such as lakes, streams, rivers, wetlands, etc. Irrigation channels are excluded from this requirement. In these buffer zones: (a) All existing native trees shall be kept, AND (b) No fertilizer and pesticides shall be used, AND (c) No logging activities shall take place, AND (d) No heavy machinery shall be used, AND (e) No cropping is allowed, AND (f) In case trees are being planted, these need to be native tree species.

expected_evidence.description: The stakeholder consultation package prepared under the Stakeholder Consultation Engagement Requirements.
expected_evidence.label: Stakeholder Consultation Record

expected_evidence.description: Evidence showing the stakeholder consultation occurred before the project start date.
expected_evidence.label: Timing Comparison

conditions: The Stakeholder Consultation is conducted before the project start date.
The consultation package follows the Stakeholder Consultation Engagement Requirements.

source_span_text: The Stakeholder Consultation shall be conducted prior to the project start date. The Project Developer shall refer to Stakeholder Consultation Engagement Requirements for further details.

expected_evidence.description: Documented verification schedule showing the AGR or A/R timing pathway that applies to the project.
expected_evidence.label: Verification Plan

expected_evidence.description: Renewal submission showing the required five-year crediting period renewal and baseline update.
expected_evidence.label: Crediting Renewal Package

conditions: For AGR projects, the first verification is completed within two years of the project start date or Project Design Certification, whichever is later.
For A/R projects, verification is completed at least every five years until the end of the crediting period.
All projects seek crediting period renewal every fifth year and update the baseline according to the applicable methodology.

source_span_text: The performance review may take place either alongside or after Project Design Certification and must occur at least once during the 5-year Certification cycle. (a) AGR specific: The first Verification shall be completed either within two years of project start date or Project Design Certification, whichever is later. (b) A/R specific: Verification shall be completed at least every 5 years until the end of the crediting period. (c) According to the Principles & Requirements, all projects shall seek Crediting Period Renewal every 5th year. At the time of project renewal, The A/R and AGR projects shall update the baseline following the applied Impact Quantification Methodology requirements.

expected_evidence.description: GIS vector files covering the required project region, project area, eligible areas, individual modelling units, infrastructure, water bodies, protected areas, biodiversity areas, and the required people-and-rights location layers where applicable.
expected_evidence.label: GIS Layer Package

expected_evidence.description: Layer naming or legend evidence showing the required comprehensive labels from clause 4.1.3 and its associated table.
expected_evidence.label: GIS Layer Labels

conditions: All projects submit GIS vector layers as specified in Table 4.
The submitted GIS vector layers are labelled comprehensively.
The layer set covers the core area, infrastructure, water body, biodiversity, affected-people, and indigenous-people or rights locations identified in clause 4.1.3.

source_span_text: All projects shall submit GIS vector layers (Table 4). The GIS vector layers shall be labelled comprehensively: Project Region; Project Area; Eligible Areas; Individual Modelling Units; Infrastructure (road, houses, etc.); Water Bodies; Protected Areas; Biodiversity Areas; Where Affected People are situated; Sites with special cultural, ecological, economic, religious or spiritual significance; Sites with special significance for indigenous people and local communities – resulting from the Local Stakeholder Consultation (LSC); Where indigenous people and local communities are situated; Where indigenous people and local communities have legal rights, customary rights or sites with special cultural, ecological, economic, religious or spiritual significance.

expected_evidence.description: Calculation package quantifying uncertainty for the LUF parameters used in the GHG estimate.
expected_evidence.label: Uncertainty Calculation

expected_evidence.description: Evidence showing whether the estimate meets the required precision target of 20% of the mean at the 90% confidence level.
expected_evidence.label: Precision Demonstration

conditions: The uncertainty treatment covers the parameters used to estimate greenhouse gas emissions and removals from the LUF activity.
The project complies with the target precision of 20% of the mean at the 90% confidence level, or applies the required deduction treatment when it does not.

source_span_text: Estimated greenhouse gas emissions and removals resulting from Land Use and Forestry (LUF) activities have uncertainties associated with the measurements/estimates of various parameters, especially area or other activity data, carbon stocks, biomass growth rates, expansion factors, emission factors and other coefficients. This guideline provides a step-by-step approach on how to treat uncertainties in LUF projects and how to comply with the required target precision of 20% of the mean at a 90% confidence level.

expected_evidence.description: Documentation showing which uncertainty approach was chosen and why it is the most specific approach possible with the available data.
expected_evidence.label: Approach Selection Basis

expected_evidence.description: The on-site measurement package or peer-reviewed literature package used when Approach 1 or Approach 2 is selected, including applicability support for the project site where relevant.
expected_evidence.label: Measurement or Literature Basis

expected_evidence.description: The default factors and associated uncertainty-deduction basis used when Approach 3 is selected.
expected_evidence.label: Default Factor and Deduction Basis

conditions: Approach 1 uses on-site measurements to directly document pre-project and project activity data.
Approach 2 uses peer-reviewed publications and requires the project owner to prove the results are conservative and applicable to the project site and management practice.
Approach 3 uses default factors and applies an uncertainty deduction if the ±20% at 90% confidence threshold is not satisfied.
The most specific approach possible with the data available is chosen.

source_span_text: To accommodate that measurements are not always available to projects, and IPCC default factors following tier 1 approach do not meet Gold Standard requirements for project data and precision level, this guideline incorporates three approaches for baseline and project activity quantification: (a) Approach 1: requires on-site measurements to directly document pre-project and project activity data. (b) Approach 2: uses peer-reviewed publications to quantify baseline and project activity data. Project owners need to prove that the research results are conservative and applicable to the project site and management practice. (c) Approach 3: applies default factors to quantify changes but a discounting factor (Uncertainty Deduction) must be applied if compliance with the uncertainty threshold of ±20% at a 90% confidence interval is not satisfied. Generally, the most specific approach possible with the data available must be chosen.

expected_evidence.description: Calculation applying the correct deduction bracket from Table 1 to the measured uncertainty range.
expected_evidence.label: Uncertainty Deduction Calculation

expected_evidence.description: Record showing how the applicable uncertainty deduction was carried into the conservative estimate used for issuance.
expected_evidence.label: Issuance Adjustment Record

conditions: If uncertainty is greater than 20% and up to 30% of the mean, a 50% uncertainty deduction is applied.
If uncertainty is greater than 30% and up to 40% of the mean, a 75% uncertainty deduction is applied.
If uncertainty is greater than 40% and up to 50% of the mean, a 100% uncertainty deduction is applied.

source_span_text: If the uncertainty is less than or equal to 20% of the mean change value then the project owner may use the estimated value without any deduction for uncertainty, i.e. UD = 0. If the uncertainty is greater than 20% of the mean value, then the project owner shall use the estimated value subject to an Uncertainty Deduction (UD) in Table 1: Table 2: Uncertainty discounting approach Uncertainty [U] Uncertainty Deduction [UD] (% of U) 20<U≤30% 50% 30<U≤40% 75% 40<U≤50% 100%.
